collisions.paper: Wei et al., 2025. Accelerating Diffusion Large Language Models with SlowFast Sampling: The Three Golden Principles. arXiv:2506.10848
collisions.overlap: SlowFast explicitly composes adaptive step scheduling with dLLM-Cache (KV caching), achieving 34.22x combined speedup on LLaDA. This is a 2-axis composition with empirical evaluation of combined speedup.

collisions.paper: Wu et al., 2025. Fast-dLLM: Training-free Acceleration of Diffusion LLM by Enabling KV Cache and Parallel Decoding. arXiv:2505.22618 (ICLR 2026)
collisions.overlap: Combines block-wise KV caching with confidence-aware parallel decoding, reporting 27.6x combined speedup. Explicitly claims the two methods are 'highly complementary.'

collisions.paper: dInfer, 2025. An Efficient Inference Framework for Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2510.08666
collisions.overlap: Decomposes dLLM inference into four modular components and tests different combinations. Partial composition framework at system level.

collisions.paper: Kim et al., 2025. KLASS: KL-Guided Fast Inference in Masked Diffusion Models. arXiv:2511.05664 (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight)
collisions.overlap: Uses token-level KL divergence between consecutive timesteps for acceleration. IGSD uses inter-step KL divergence for step scheduling. Same core insight (KL as temporal stability signal), different granularity.

collisions.paper: Sedykh et al., 2026. Not All Denoising Steps Are Equal: Model Scheduling for Faster Masked Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2604.02340
collisions.overlap: Measures step importance via KL divergence between small/large models across timesteps. Identifies 'inverted-U' profile matching IGSD's H6 hypothesis. Same core signal, different application (model swap vs. step skip).

collisions.paper: TORS, 2026. Analyzing and Improving Training-Free Fast Sampling of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models. arXiv:2603.00763
collisions.overlap: Identical methodology for text-to-image domain: systematic design space analysis of training-free acceleration methods, composition compatibility evaluation. Same framing, different domain.

collisions.paper: arXiv:2510.18480. How Efficient Are Diffusion Language Models? A Critical Examination of Efficiency Evaluation Practices.
collisions.overlap: Systematic benchmarking of DLM efficiency claims. Notes diminishing gains at larger batch sizes. Relevant evaluation context.

collisions.paper: EntropyCache, 2026. Decoded Token Entropy Guided KV Caching for Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2603.18489
collisions.overlap: Achieves 15.2-26.4x speedup with entropy-guided KV caching. Stronger M1 axis candidate than what the proposal uses.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No existing paper provides all four: (a) controlled 3-way factorial design, (b) quantified orthogonality metric for method pairs, (c) task-specific Pareto frontiers, (d) speedup decomposition framework. However, the 'no composition study' framing is weakened by SlowFast+cache (34.22x) and Fast-dLLM (27.6x) which already combine 2 axes. IGSD novelty weakened by KLASS. Must reframe: acknowledge existing 2-axis compositions, differentiate via systematic 3+ axis factorial design and orthogonality quantification. Should rename IGSD more modestly and update method pool to include EntropyCache, LogicDiff, Saber as candidates.

collisions.paper: Chen & Cai, 2025. A Convergence Theory for Diffusion Language Models: An Information-Theoretic Perspective. arXiv:2505.21400
collisions.overlap: Establishes convergence guarantees for DLMs from information-theoretic perspective. Proves KL divergence decays as O(1/T), scales linearly with mutual information. Matching upper and lower bounds. This is the exact core contribution claimed.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2025. Optimal Inference Schedules for Masked Diffusion Models. arXiv:2511.04647
collisions.overlap: Provides optimal scheduling bounds in terms of total correlation and dual total correlation. Shows O(log n) step sampling possible. Directly addresses optimal scheduling from information-theoretic perspective.

collisions.paper: Kim et al., 2025. KLASS: KL-Guided Fast Inference in Masked Diffusion Models. arXiv:2511.05664 (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight)
collisions.overlap: Empirically demonstrates KL divergence as per-token convergence signal. Validates H5 (entropy/KL as proxy for convergence budget) empirically.

collisions.paper: DyLLM (2603.08026), ES-dLLM (2603.10088), Dynamic-dLLM — Per-token adaptive compute frameworks
collisions.overlap: All implement per-token adaptive compute allocation based on various convergence signals. The methods the proposal claims to 'unify' already work without the theory.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Core contribution (information-theoretic convergence bounds for masked diffusion LMs) has been published by Chen & Cai (2505.21400) with matching upper/lower bounds. Optimal scheduling bounds also published (2511.04647). Remaining novelty limited to specific water-filling allocation and formal proof of entropy optimality among proxies, which is incremental over existing work.

collisions.paper: Ni et al., 2026. The Flexibility Trap: Why Arbitrary Order Limits Reasoning Potential in Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2601.15165
collisions.overlap: Directly identifies the same problem: confidence-based unmasking order is fundamentally flawed for reasoning. Shows order flexibility narrows reasoning boundary. This IS the core thesis of cand_order_first.

collisions.paper: Shaik et al., 2026. LogicDiff: Logic-Guided Denoising Improves Reasoning in Masked Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2603.26771
collisions.overlap: Proposes logic-role-guided unmasking. Achieves +38.7pp on GSM8K (22.0% to 60.7%) with <6% speed overhead. SOLVES the problem cand_order_first proposes to address, with massive empirical results far exceeding the proposal's H8 target (>10pp).

collisions.paper: Olausson et al., 2025. Learning Unmasking Policies for Diffusion Language Models. arXiv:2512.09106 (ICLR 2026 DeLTa Workshop Oral)
collisions.overlap: Trains unmasking policies via RL. Different approach (RL vs. training-free entropy inversion) but same problem (learning better unmasking order).

collisions.paper: Ground-Truth-Guided Unmasking Order Learning, 2026. arXiv:2602.09501
collisions.overlap: Addresses train-inference mismatch in unmasking order. Same problem framing, training-based solution.

collisions.paper: TRIMS: Trajectory-Ranked Instruction Masked Supervision, 2026. arXiv:2604.00666
collisions.overlap: Provides explicit supervision over token reveal order during training. Same gap identified, training-based solution.

candidates.differentiation_notes: The Flexibility Trap identifies the exact problem. LogicDiff solves it with +38.7pp on GSM8K, far exceeding the proposal's >10pp target. Multiple concurrent works (RL policies, ground-truth-guided, TRIMS) attack the same problem. The remaining angle (does corrected order improve acceleration composition?) is thin and would be a follow-up study dependent on LogicDiff.

collisions.paper: Various classical works. Multigrid methods for PDE-based image denoising (Rudin-Osher-Fatemi 1992, etc.)
collisions.overlap: Classical multigrid V-cycle inspiration. Well-established for continuous PDEs, not for discrete masked diffusion.

collisions.paper: DyLLM, 2026. Efficient Diffusion LLM Inference via Saliency-based Token Selection and Partial Attention. arXiv:2603.08026
collisions.overlap: Identifies salient tokens via cosine similarity, recomputes only those while caching the rest. Functionally similar to restrict-correct cycle (identify uncertain tokens, compute only those, cache rest). Achieves 9.6x throughput. But lacks multigrid framing and principled hierarchical decomposition.

collisions.paper: ES-dLLM, 2026. Efficient Inference for Diffusion Large Language Models by Early-Skipping. arXiv:2603.10088
collisions.overlap: Skips tokens in early layers based on estimated importance. Related to restriction concept but at layer level, not V-cycle.

collisions.paper: Token merging / hierarchical attention in DiT literature (CVPR 2025, NeurIPS 2024 works)
collisions.overlap: Token merging and hierarchical processing for text-to-image Diffusion Transformers. Different domain and mechanism.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No existing work frames DLM denoising as a multigrid V-cycle. DyLLM achieves similar practical outcome (selective token compute + caching) but lacks the theoretical multigrid framework and principled smooth/restrict/correct/prolongate/post-smooth decomposition. Highest genuine novelty among all candidates. Main risk is feasibility (score 5) and whether the V-cycle adds practical value beyond DyLLM.

critical_findings: SlowFast Sampling (Jun 2025) already composes step scheduling + KV caching achieving 34.22x on LLaDA — weakens ComposeAccel's 'no composition study' framing
KLASS (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight) uses the same KL divergence signal for DLM acceleration that IGSD claims as novel — must acknowledge and differentiate
Convergence theory candidate is directly preempted by Chen & Cai (2505.21400) with tight information-theoretic bounds
LogicDiff achieves +38.7pp on GSM8K via unmasking order correction — completely supersedes cand_order_first with far stronger results
The field has moved extremely fast: 20+ training-free DLM acceleration papers in Q1 2026 alone
EntropyCache (Mar 2026) achieves 15-26x speedup — should be included in ComposeAccel's method pool
No paper yet provides a controlled factorial composition study with quantified orthogonality metrics across 3+ axes — this gap still holds for ComposeAccel
